facts.text: Apollo Beach earned its name in 1958, inspired by the Greek sun god, after developer Paul Dickman renamed the area from Tampa Beach to La Vida Beach.
The Big Bend Power Station, a landmark in Apollo Beach, supplies electricity to the entire Tampa Bay area and sits on land once owned by the Dickman family.
Before becoming a residential community, the land that is now Apollo Beach was used for farming and grazing pasture by the Dickman family in the 1920s.
Apollo Beach lies just 17 miles south of downtown Tampa, with Tampa Bay forming its western boundary and the Big Bend Power Station marking its northern edge.
The area that became Apollo Beach was originally part of the Tocobaga people’s territory, who lived along the mangrove‑lined shores of Tampa Bay for over a thousand years.
In the 1950s, developer Paul Dickman began subdividing the 5,500‑acre tract, eventually creating the Apollo Beach community that thrives today.
